title: platostoma axillaris
intro: Platostoma axillaris, also known as the African Blue Basil or Blue Plectranthus, is a species of flowering plant in the Lamiaceae family, native to tropical Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Platostoma axillaris include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and even exhibit antiviral and antibacterial properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. In terms of herbal preparations, Platostoma axillaris is often used as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in topical applications, such as creams or ointments, to take advantage of its therapeutic properties.
